     258              : /// @} dnnl_api_primitives_common
     259              : 
     260              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_attributes
     261              : ///
     262              : /// A container for parameters that extend primitives behavior.
     263              : ///
     264              : /// Attributes can also contain Post-ops, which are computations executed
     265              : /// after the primitive.
     266              : ///
     267              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_attributes
     268              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_attributes_post_ops
     269              : ///
     270              : /// @{
     271              : 
     272              : /// Scratchpad mode
     273              : enum class scratchpad_mode {
     274              :     /// The library manages the scratchpad allocation according to the policy
     275              :     /// specified by the `DNNL_ENABLE_CONCURRENT_EXEC`
     276              :     /// [build option](@ref dev_guide_build_options) (default).
     277              :     ///
     278              :     /// When `DNNL_ENABLE_CONCURRENT_EXEC=OFF` (default), the library
     279              :     /// scratchpad is common to all primitives to reduce the memory footprint.
     280              :     /// This configuration comes with limited thread-safety properties, namely
     281              :     /// primitives can be created and executed in parallel but cannot migrate
     282              :     /// between threads (in other words, each primitive should be executed in
     283              :     /// the same thread it was created in).
     284              :     ///
     285              :     /// When `DNNL_ENABLE_CONCURRENT_EXEC=ON`, the library scratchpad is
     286              :     /// private to each primitive. The memory footprint is larger than when
     287              :     /// using `DNNL_ENABLE_CONCURRENT_EXEC=OFF` but different primitives can be
     288              :     /// created and run concurrently (the same primitive cannot be run
     289              :     /// concurrently from two different threads though).
     290              :     library = dnnl_scratchpad_mode_library,
     291              :     /// The user manages the scratchpad allocation by querying and providing
     292              :     /// the scratchpad memory to primitives. This mode is thread-safe as long
     293              :     /// as the scratchpad buffers are not used concurrently by two primitive
     294              :     /// executions.
     295              :     user = dnnl_scratchpad_mode_user,
     296              : };
     297              : 
     298              : /// Converts a scratchpad mode enum value from C++ API to C API type.
     299              : ///
     300              : /// @param mode C++ API scratchpad mode enum value.
     301              : /// @returns Corresponding C API scratchpad mode enum value.
     302       238429 : inline dnnl_scratchpad_mode_t convert_to_c(scratchpad_mode mode) {
     303       238429 :     return static_cast<dnnl_scratchpad_mode_t>(mode);
     304              : }

     306              : /// Rounding mode
     307              : enum class rounding_mode {
     308              :     /// rounding mode dictated by the floating-point environment
     309              :     environment = dnnl_rounding_mode_environment,
     310              :     /// stochastic rounding mode where a random bias is added to the
     311              :     /// trailing mantissa bits before conversion.
     312              :     stochastic = dnnl_rounding_mode_stochastic
     313              : };

     323              : /// Quantization kind
     324              : enum class quantization_mode {
     325              :     /// used for unspecified quantization kind
     326              :     undef = dnnl_quantization_mode_undef,
     327              :     /// static quantization mode: quantization parameter is computed
     328              :     /// ahead of time and passed to oneDNN as an input.
     329              :     static_sazp = dnnl_quantization_mode_static_sazp,
     330              :     /// dynamic quantization mode following OCP MX spec: quantization
     331              :     /// parameter is computed by oneDNN following the OCP MX spec
     332              :     /// formula and written as an output.
     333              :     dynamic_mx = dnnl_quantization_mode_dynamic_mx,
     334              :     /// dynamic quantization mode where quantization parameter is computed by
     335              :     /// oneDNN as \f$scale\_dt(amax(X) / max(dst\_dt))\f$ in `f32` then
     336              :     /// converted to a scale type and written as an output.
     337              :     dynamic_fp = dnnl_quantization_mode_dynamic_fp,
     338              : };

     348              : /// Propagation kind.
     349              : enum class prop_kind {
     350              :     /// Undefined propagation kind.
     351              :     undef = dnnl_prop_kind_undef,
     352              :     /// Forward data propagation (training mode). In this mode, primitives
     353              :     /// perform computations necessary for subsequent backward propagation.
     354              :     forward_training = dnnl_forward_training,
     355              :     /// Forward data propagation (inference mode). In this mode, primitives
     356              :     /// perform only computations that are necessary for inference and omit
     357              :     /// computations that are necessary only for backward propagation.
     358              :     forward_inference = dnnl_forward_inference,
     359              :     /// Forward data propagation,
     360              :     /// alias for #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training.
     361              :     forward = dnnl_forward,
     362              :     /// Backward propagation (with respect to all parameters).
     363              :     backward = dnnl_backward,
     364              :     /// Backward data propagation.
     365              :     backward_data = dnnl_backward_data,
     366              :     /// Backward weights propagation.
     367              :     backward_weights = dnnl_backward_weights,
     368              :     /// Backward bias propagation.
     369              :     backward_bias = dnnl_backward_bias
     370              : };

     380              : /// Kinds of algorithms.

     451              :     /// Local response normalization (LRN) across multiple channels

     453              :     /// LRN within a single channel
     454              :     lrn_within_channel = dnnl_lrn_within_channel,
     455              :     /// Max pooling
     456              :     pooling_max = dnnl_pooling_max,
     457              :     /// Average pooling include padding
     458              :     pooling_avg_include_padding = dnnl_pooling_avg_include_padding,
     459              :     /// Average pooling exclude padding
     460              :     pooling_avg_exclude_padding = dnnl_pooling_avg_exclude_padding,
     461              :     /// RNN cell

     463              :     /// LSTM cell
     464              :     vanilla_lstm = dnnl_vanilla_lstm,
     465              :     /// GRU cell
     466              :     vanilla_gru = dnnl_vanilla_gru,
     467              :     /// GRU cell with linear before reset. Differs from the vanilla GRU
     468              :     /// in how the new memory gate is calculated:
     469              :     /// \f$c_t = tanh(W_c*x_t + b_{c_x} + r_t*(U_c*h_{t-1}+b_{c_h})) \f$
     470              :     /// LRB GRU expects 4 bias tensors on input:
     471              :     /// \f$[b_{u}, b_{r}, b_{c_x}, b_{c_h}]\f$
     472              :     lbr_gru = dnnl_lbr_gru,
     473              :     /// AUGRU cell
     474              :     vanilla_augru = dnnl_vanilla_augru,
     475              :     /// AUGRU cell with linear before reset
     476              :     lbr_augru = dnnl_lbr_augru,
     477              :     /// Binary add
     478              :     binary_add = dnnl_binary_add,
     479              :     /// Binary mul
     480              :     binary_mul = dnnl_binary_mul,
     481              :     /// Binary max
     482              :     binary_max = dnnl_binary_max,
     483              :     /// Binary min
     484              :     binary_min = dnnl_binary_min,
     485              :     /// Binary div
     486              :     binary_div = dnnl_binary_div,
     487              :     /// Binary sub
     488              :     binary_sub = dnnl_binary_sub,
     489              :     /// Binary greater than or equal
     490              :     binary_ge = dnnl_binary_ge,
     491              :     /// Binary greater than
     492              :     binary_gt = dnnl_binary_gt,
     493              :     /// Binary less than or equal
     494              :     binary_le = dnnl_binary_le,
     495              :     /// Binary less than
     496              :     binary_lt = dnnl_binary_lt,
     497              :     /// Binary equal
     498              :     binary_eq = dnnl_binary_eq,
     499              :     /// Binary not equal
     500              :     binary_ne = dnnl_binary_ne,
     501              :     /// Binary select
     502              :     binary_select = dnnl_binary_select,
     503              :     /// Nearest Neighbor resampling method
     504              :     resampling_nearest = dnnl_resampling_nearest,
     505              :     /// Linear (Bilinear, Trilinear) resampling method
     506              :     resampling_linear = dnnl_resampling_linear,
     507              :     /// Reduction using max operation
     508              :     reduction_max = dnnl_reduction_max,
     509              :     /// Reduction using min operation
     510              :     reduction_min = dnnl_reduction_min,
     511              :     /// Reduction using sum operation
     512              :     reduction_sum = dnnl_reduction_sum,
     513              :     /// Reduction using mul operation
     514              :     reduction_mul = dnnl_reduction_mul,
     515              :     /// Reduction using mean operation
     516              :     reduction_mean = dnnl_reduction_mean,
     517              :     /// Reduction using norm_lp_max operation
     518              :     reduction_norm_lp_max = dnnl_reduction_norm_lp_max,
     519              :     /// Reduction using norm_lp_sum operation
     520              :     reduction_norm_lp_sum = dnnl_reduction_norm_lp_sum,
     521              :     /// Reduction using norm_lp_power_p_max operation
     522              :     reduction_norm_lp_power_p_max = dnnl_reduction_norm_lp_power_p_max,
     523              :     /// Reduction using norm_lp_power_p_sum operation
     524              :     reduction_norm_lp_power_p_sum = dnnl_reduction_norm_lp_power_p_sum,
     525              :     /// Softmax, numerically stable
     526              :     softmax_accurate = dnnl_softmax_accurate,
     527              :     /// LogSoftmax, numerically stable
     528              :     softmax_log = dnnl_softmax_log,
     529              : };
     530              : 
     531              : /// Converts algorithm kind enum value from C++ API to C API type.
     532              : /// @param aalgorithm C++ API algorithm kind enum value.
     533              : /// @returns Corresponding C API algorithm kind enum value.
     534      1489297 : inline dnnl_alg_kind_t convert_to_c(algorithm aalgorithm) {
     535      1489297 :     return static_cast<dnnl_alg_kind_t>(aalgorithm);
     536              : }
     537              : 
     538              : /// @} dnnl_api_attributes
     539              : 
     540              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_primitives_common
     541              : /// @{
     542              : 
     543              : /// Flags for normalization primitives.
     544              : enum class normalization_flags : unsigned {
     545              :     /// Use no normalization flags. If specified, the library computes mean and
     546              :     /// variance on forward propagation for training and inference, outputs
     547              :     /// them on forward propagation for training, and computes the respective
     548              :     /// derivatives on backward propagation.
     549              :     ///
     550              :     /// @note
     551              :     ///     Backward propagation of type #dnnl::prop_kind::backward_data has
     552              :     ///     the same behavior as #dnnl::prop_kind::backward.
     553              :     none = dnnl_normalization_flags_none,
     554              : 
     555              :     /// Use global statistics. If specified, the library uses mean and
     556              :     /// variance provided by the user as an input on forward propagation and
     557              :     /// does not compute their derivatives on backward propagation. Otherwise,
     558              :     /// the library computes mean and variance on forward propagation for
     559              :     /// training and inference, outputs them on forward propagation for
     560              :     /// training, and computes the respective derivatives on backward
     561              :     /// propagation.
     562              :     use_global_stats = dnnl_use_global_stats,
     563              : 
     564              :     /// Use scale parameter. If specified, the user is expected to pass scale as
     565              :     /// input on forward propagation. On backward propagation of type
     566              :     /// #dnnl::prop_kind::backward, the library computes its derivative.
     567              :     use_scale = dnnl_use_scale,
     568              : 
     569              :     /// Use shift parameter. If specified, the user is expected to pass shift as
     570              :     /// input on forward propagation. On backward propagation of type
     571              :     /// #dnnl::prop_kind::backward, the library computes its derivative.
     572              :     use_shift = dnnl_use_shift,
     573              : 
     574              :     /// Fuse normalization with ReLU. On training, normalization will require
     575              :     /// the workspace to implement backward propagation. On inference, the
     576              :     /// workspace is not required and behavior is the same as when normalization
     577              :     /// is fused with ReLU using the post-ops API.
     578              :     ///
     579              :     /// @note
     580              :     ///     The flag implies negative slope being 0. On training this is the only
     581              :     ///     configuration supported. For inference, to use non-zero negative slope
     582              :     ///     consider using @ref dev_guide_attributes_post_ops.
     583              :     fuse_norm_relu = dnnl_fuse_norm_relu,
     584              : 
     585              :     /// Fuse normalization with an elementwise binary Add operation
     586              :     /// followed by ReLU.
     587              :     /// During training, normalization will require a workspace to implement
     588              :     /// backward propagation. For inference, the workspace is not needed.
     589              :     /// On forward propagation, an elementwise binary Add operation is applied
     590              :     /// to the normalization results with an additional input tensor, followed
     591              :     /// by ReLU with a negative slope of 0.
     592              :     /// On backward propagation, the result of the backward ReLU operation
     593              :     /// with the input tensor and workspace from the forward pass is saved
     594              :     /// to an extra output tensor, and backward normalization is performed.
     595              :     fuse_norm_add_relu = dnnl_fuse_norm_add_relu,
     596              : 
     597              :     /// Use Root Mean Square (RMS) Normalization. In forward propagation,
     598              :     /// the mean is considered zero, and RMS norm is used instead of variance
     599              :     /// for scaling. Only the RMS norm is output during forward propagation for
     600              :     /// training. In backward propagation, the library calculates the derivative
     601              :     /// with respect to the RMS norm only, assuming the mean is zero.
     602              :     ///
     603              :     /// @note
     604              :     ///     When used with #dnnl::normalization_flags::use_global_stats,
     605              :     ///     only RMS norm is required to be provided as input.
     606              :     rms_norm = dnnl_rms_norm,
     607              : };
     608              : 
     609              : /// Converts normalization flags enum value from C++ API to C API type.
     610              : /// @param flags C++ API normalization flags enum value.
     611              : /// @returns Corresponding C API normalization flags enum value.
     612       702823 : inline dnnl_normalization_flags_t convert_to_c(normalization_flags flags) {
     613       702823 :     return static_cast<dnnl_normalization_flags_t>(flags);
     614              : }
     615              :

     617              : 
     618              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_rnn
     619              : /// @{
     620              : 
     621              : /// RNN cell flags.
     622              : enum class rnn_flags : unsigned {
     623              :     /// Undefined RNN flags
     624              :     undef = dnnl_rnn_flags_undef,
     625              :     /// Do not add weights gradient to existing diff_weights memory
     626              :     diff_weights_overwrite = dnnl_rnn_flags_diff_weights_overwrite,
     627              : };
     628              : 
     629              : /// Converts RNN cell flags enum value from C++ API to C API type.

     631              : /// @returns Corresponding C API RNN cell flags enum value.
     632         1889 : inline dnnl_rnn_flags_t convert_to_c(rnn_flags flags) {
     633         1889 :     return static_cast<dnnl_rnn_flags_t>(flags);
     634              : }
     635              : 
     636       193207 : DNNL_DEFINE_BITMASK_OPS(normalization_flags)
     637              : DNNL_DEFINE_BITMASK_OPS(rnn_flags)
     638              : 
     639              : /// A direction of RNN primitive execution
     640              : enum class rnn_direction {
     641              :     /// Undefined RNN direction.
     642              :     undef = dnnl_rnn_direction_undef,
     643              :     /// Unidirectional execution of RNN primitive from left to right.
     644              :     unidirectional_left2right = dnnl_unidirectional_left2right,
     645              :     /// Unidirectional execution of RNN primitive from right to left.
     646              :     unidirectional_right2left = dnnl_unidirectional_right2left,
     647              :     /// Bidirectional execution of RNN primitive with concatenation of the
     648              :     /// results.
     649              :     bidirectional_concat = dnnl_bidirectional_concat,
     650              :     /// Bidirectional execution of RNN primitive with summation of the
     651              :     /// results.
     652              :     bidirectional_sum = dnnl_bidirectional_sum,
     653              : };
     654              : 
     655              : /// Converts RNN direction enum value from C++ API to C API type.
     656              : /// @param dir C++ API RNN direction enum value.
     657              : /// @returns Corresponding C API RNN direction enum value.
     658         1889 : inline dnnl_rnn_direction_t convert_to_c(rnn_direction dir) {
     659         1889 :     return static_cast<dnnl_rnn_direction_t>(dir);
     660              : }
     661              : 
     662              : /// @} dnnl_api_rnn
     663              : 
     664              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_primitives_common
     665              : /// @{
     666              : 
     667              : /// Primitive descriptor query specification.
     668              : ///
     669              : /// In general, queries are not used with the C++ API because most queries are
     670              : /// implemented as class members.
     671              : ///
     672              : /// See @ref dnnl_query_t for more information.
     673              : enum class query {
     674              :     /// no query
     675              :     undef = dnnl_query_undef,
     676              : 
     677              :     /// execution engine
     678              :     engine = dnnl_query_engine,
     679              :     /// primitive kind
     680              :     primitive_kind = dnnl_query_primitive_kind,
     681              : 
     682              :     /// number of inputs expected
     683              :     num_of_inputs_s32 = dnnl_query_num_of_inputs_s32,
     684              :     /// number of outputs expected
     685              :     num_of_outputs_s32 = dnnl_query_num_of_outputs_s32,
     686              : 
     687              :     /// runtime estimation (seconds), unimplemented
     688              :     time_estimate_f64 = dnnl_query_time_estimate_f64,
     689              :     /// memory required for scratchpad (bytes)
     690              :     ///
     691              :     /// @sa @ref dev_guide_attributes_scratchpad
     692              :     memory_consumption_s64 = dnnl_query_memory_consumption_s64,
     693              : 
     694              :     /// scratchpad engine
     695              :     ///
     696              :     /// engine to be used for creating scratchpad memory
     697              :     scratchpad_engine = dnnl_query_scratchpad_engine,
     698              : 
     699              :     /// reorder source engine
     700              :     reorder_src_engine = dnnl_query_reorder_src_engine,
     701              :     /// reorder destination engine
     702              :     reorder_dst_engine = dnnl_query_reorder_dst_engine,
     703              : 
     704              :     /// implementation name
     705              :     impl_info_str = dnnl_query_impl_info_str,
     706              : 
     707              :     /// propagation kind
     708              :     prop_kind = dnnl_query_prop_kind,
     709              : 
     710              :     /// size of cache blob ID in bytes
     711              :     cache_blob_id_size_s64 = dnnl_query_cache_blob_id_size_s64,
     712              : 
     713              :     /// cache blob ID (pointer to array)
     714              :     cache_blob_id = dnnl_query_cache_blob_id,
     715              : 
     716              :     /// strides
     717              :     strides = dnnl_query_strides,
     718              :     /// dilations
     719              :     dilations = dnnl_query_dilations,
     720              :     /// left padding
     721              :     padding_l = dnnl_query_padding_l,
     722              :     /// right padding
     723              :     padding_r = dnnl_query_padding_r,
     724              :     /// epsilon
     725              :     epsilon_f32 = dnnl_query_epsilon_f32,
     726              :     /// flags
     727              :     flags = dnnl_query_flags,
     728              :     /// algorithm kind
     729              :     alg_kind = dnnl_query_alg_kind,
     730              :     /// alpha
     731              :     alpha_f32 = dnnl_query_alpha_f32,
     732              :     /// beta
     733              :     beta_f32 = dnnl_query_beta_f32,
     734              :     /// axis
     735              :     axis_s32 = dnnl_query_axis_s32,
     736              :     /// LRN parameter local size
     737              :     local_size_s64 = dnnl_query_local_size_s64,
     738              :     /// LRN parameter K
     739              :     k_f32 = dnnl_query_k_f32,
     740              :     /// Reduction parameter P
     741              :     p_f32 = dnnl_query_p_f32,
     742              :     /// Resampling parameter factors
     743              :     factors = dnnl_query_factors,
     744              :     /// RNN parameter cell kind
     745              :     cell_kind = dnnl_query_cell_kind,
     746              :     /// RNN parameter direction
     747              :     direction = dnnl_query_direction,
     748              :     /// RNN parameter activation kind
     749              :     activation_kind = dnnl_query_activation_kind,
     750              :     /// Pooling parameter kernel
     751              :     kernel = dnnl_query_kernel,
     752              :     /// Shuffle parameter group size
     753              :     group_size_s64 = dnnl_query_group_size_s64,
     754              : 
     755              :     /// source memory desc
     756              :     src_md = dnnl_query_src_md,
     757              :     /// source gradient (diff) memory desc
     758              :     diff_src_md = dnnl_query_diff_src_md,
     759              :     /// weights memory descriptor desc
     760              :     weights_md = dnnl_query_weights_md,
     761              :     /// weights gradient (diff) memory desc
     762              :     diff_weights_md = dnnl_query_diff_weights_md,
     763              :     /// destination memory desc
     764              :     dst_md = dnnl_query_dst_md,
     765              :     /// destination gradient (diff) memory desc
     766              :     diff_dst_md = dnnl_query_diff_dst_md,
     767              :     /// workspace memory desc
     768              :     workspace_md = dnnl_query_workspace_md,
     769              :     /// scratchpad memory desc
     770              :     scratchpad_md = dnnl_query_scratchpad_md,
     771              :     /// memory desc of an execute argument
     772              :     exec_arg_md = dnnl_query_exec_arg_md,
     773              : 
     774              :     /// number of dimensions
     775              :     ndims_s32 = dnnl_query_ndims_s32,
     776              :     /// vector of dimensions
     777              :     dims = dnnl_query_dims,
     778              :     /// data type
     779              :     data_type = dnnl_query_data_type,
     780              :     /// submemory offset
     781              :     submemory_offset_s64 = dnnl_query_submemory_offset_s64,
     782              :     /// vector of padded dimensions
     783              :     padded_dims = dnnl_query_padded_dims,
     784              :     /// vector of padded offsets
     785              :     padded_offsets = dnnl_query_padded_offsets,
     786              :     /// format kind
     787              :     format_kind = dnnl_query_format_kind,
     788              :     ///  number of innermost blocks
     789              :     inner_nblks_s32 = dnnl_query_inner_nblks_s32,
     790              :     /// vector of sizes of the innermost blocks
     791              :     inner_blks = dnnl_query_inner_blks,
     792              :     /// vector of logical indices of the blocks
     793              :     inner_idxs = dnnl_query_inner_idxs,
     794              :     /// Sparse encoding
     795              :     sparse_encoding = dnnl_query_sparse_encoding,
     796              :     /// Number of non-zero entries
     797              :     nnz_s64 = dnnl_query_nnz_s64,
     798              :     /// Number of buffers required for a memory descriptor
     799              :     num_handles_s32 = dnnl_query_num_handles_s32,
     800              : };
     801              : 
     802              : /// Converts query enum value from C++ API to C API type.
     803              : /// @param aquery C++ API query enum value.
     804              : /// @returns Corresponding C API query enum value.
     805   2894653810 : inline dnnl_query_t convert_to_c(query aquery) {

     808              : 
     809              : /// @} dnnl_api_primitives_common
     810              : 
     811              : /// @} dnnl_api_primitives
     812              : 
     813              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_memory Memory
     814              : ///
     815              : /// A container that describes and stores data. Memory objects can contain
     816              : /// data of various types and formats. There are two levels of abstraction:
     817              : ///
     818              : /// 1. **Memory descriptor** -- engine-agnostic logical description of data
     819              : ///     (number of dimensions, dimension sizes, and data type), and,
     820              : ///     optionally, the information about the physical format of data in
     821              : ///     memory. If this information is not known yet, a memory descriptor can
     822              : ///     be created with #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any. This allows
     823              : ///     compute-intensive primitives to choose the best format for
     824              : ///     computation. The user is responsible for reordering the data into the
     825              : ///     chosen format when formats do not match.
     826              : ///
     827              : ///     A memory descriptor can be initialized either by specifying dimensions
     828              : ///     and a memory format tag or strides for each of them, or by
     829              : ///     manipulating the dnnl_memory_desc_t structure directly.
     830              : ///
     831              : ///     @warning
     832              : ///         The latter approach requires understanding how the physical data
     833              : ///         representation is mapped to the structure and is discouraged. This
     834              : ///         topic is discussed in @ref dev_guide_understanding_memory_formats.
     835              : ///
     836              : ///     The user can query the amount of memory required by a memory
     837              : ///     descriptor using the #dnnl::memory::desc::get_size() function. The
     838              : ///     size of data in general cannot be computed as the product of
     839              : ///     dimensions multiplied by the size of the data type. So users are
     840              : ///     required to use this function for better code portability.
     841              : ///
     842              : ///     Two memory descriptors can be compared using the equality and
     843              : ///     inequality operators.  The comparison is especially useful when
     844              : ///     checking whether it is necessary to reorder data from the user's data
     845              : ///     format to a primitive's format.
     846              : ///
     847              : /// 2. **Memory object** -- an engine-specific object that handles the memory
     848              : ///     buffer and its description (a memory descriptor). For the CPU engine or
     849              : ///     with USM, the memory buffer handle is simply a pointer to @c void. The
     850              : ///     memory buffer can be queried using #dnnl::memory::get_data_handle() and
     851              : ///     set using #dnnl::memory::set_data_handle(). The underlying SYCL buffer,
     852              : ///     when used, can be queried using #dnnl::sycl_interop::get_buffer and set
     853              : ///     using #dnnl::sycl_interop::set_buffer. A memory object can also be
     854              : ///     queried for the underlying memory descriptor and for its engine using
     855              : ///     #dnnl::memory::get_desc() and dnnl::memory::get_engine().
     856              : ///
     857              : /// Along with ordinary memory descriptors with all dimensions being positive,
     858              : /// the library supports *zero-volume*  memory descriptors with one or more
     859              : /// dimensions set to zero. This is used to support the NumPy\* convention.
     860              : /// If a zero-volume memory is passed to a primitive, the primitive typically
     861              : /// does not perform any computations with this memory. For example:
     862              : ///
     863              : /// - A concatenation primitive would ignore all memory object with zeroes in
     864              : ///   the concat dimension / axis.
     865              : ///
     866              : /// - A forward convolution with a source memory object with zero in the
     867              : ///   minibatch dimension would always produce a destination memory object
     868              : ///   with a zero in the minibatch dimension and perform no computations.
     869              : ///
     870              : /// - However, a forward convolution with a zero in one of the weights
     871              : ///   dimensions is ill-defined and is considered to be an error by the
     872              : ///   library because there is no clear definition of what the output values
     873              : ///   should be.
     874              : ///
     875              : /// Memory buffer of a zero-volume memory is never accessed.
     876              : ///
     877              : /// @{
     878              : 
     879              : /// Memory object.
     880              : ///
     881              : /// A memory object encapsulates a handle to a memory buffer allocated on a
     882              : /// specific engine, tensor dimensions, data type, and memory format, which is
     883              : /// the way tensor indices map to offsets in linear memory space. Memory
     884              : /// objects are passed to primitives during execution.
     885     15687889 : struct memory : public handle<dnnl_memory_t> {
     886       124101 :     using handle::handle;
     887              : 
     888              :     /// Integer type for representing dimension sizes and indices.
     889              :     using dim = dnnl_dim_t;
     890              :     /// Vector of dimensions. Implementations are free to force a limit on the
     891              :     /// vector's length.
     892              :     using dims = std::vector<dim>;
     893              : 
     894              :     /// Helper function that validates that an `std::vector` of dimensions can
     895              :     /// be safely converted to the C API array ::dnnl_dims_t. Throws if
     896              :     /// validation fails.
     897              :     ///
     898              :     /// @param v Vector of dimensions.
     899              :     /// @param min_size Minimum expected size of the vector.
     900              :     template <typename T>
     901    147893795 :     static void validate_dims(const std::vector<T> &v, int min_size = 0) {
     902    144804839 :         validate_container_size(
     903              :                 v, "dimensions are invalid", min_size, DNNL_MAX_NDIMS);
     904    147890469 :     }
     905              : 
     906              :     /// Data type specification.
     907              :     enum class data_type {
     908              :         /// Undefined data type (used for empty memory descriptors).
     909              :         undef = dnnl_data_type_undef,
     910              :         /// 4-bit float data type with 3-bit exponent and 0 bit mantissa.
     911              :         ///
     912              :         /// @deprecated The f4_e3m0 data type will be removed in a future release.
     913              :         f4_e3m0 = dnnl_f4_e3m0,
     914              :         /// [MX-compliant 4-bit float data type](https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-microscaling-formats-mx-v1-0-spec-final-pdf) with 2-bit exponent and 1 bit mantissa.
     915              :         f4_e2m1 = dnnl_f4_e2m1,
     916              :         /// [MX-compliant 8-bit compliant scale data type](https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-microscaling-formats-mx-v1-0-spec-final-pdf) with 8-bit exponent.
     917              :         e8m0 = dnnl_e8m0,
     918              :         /// [OFP8 standard 8-bit floating-point](https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-8-bit-floating-point-specification-ofp8-revision-1-0-2023-06-20-pdf)
     919              :         /// with a 5-bit exponent and a 2-bit mantissa.
     920              :         f8_e5m2 = dnnl_f8_e5m2,
     921              :         /// [OFP8 standard 8-bit floating-point](https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-8-bit-floating-point-specification-ofp8-revision-1-0-2023-06-20-pdf)
     922              :         /// with a 4-bit exponent and a 3-bit mantissa.
     923              :         f8_e4m3 = dnnl_f8_e4m3,
     924              :         /// [16-bit/half-precision floating point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Half-precision_floating-point_format).
     925              :         f16 = dnnl_f16,
     926              :         /// non-standard
     927              :         /// [16-bit floating point with 7-bit mantissa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bfloat16_floating-point_format).
     928              :         bf16 = dnnl_bf16,
     929              :         /// [32-bit/single-precision floating point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single-precision_floating-point_format).
     930              :         f32 = dnnl_f32,
     931              :         //// [64-bit/double-precision floating point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Double-precision_floating-point_format).
     932              :         f64 = dnnl_f64,
     933              :         /// 64-bit signed integer
     934              :         s64 = dnnl_s64,
     935              :         /// 32-bit signed integer.
     936              :         s32 = dnnl_s32,
     937              :         /// 8-bit signed integer.
     938              :         s8 = dnnl_s8,
     939              :         /// 8-bit unsigned integer.
     940              :         u8 = dnnl_u8,
     941              :         /// 4-bit signed integer.
     942              :         s4 = dnnl_s4,
     943              :         /// 4-bit unsigned integer.
     944              :         u4 = dnnl_u4,
     945              :     };
     946              : 
     947              :     /// Returns size of data type in bytes.
     948              :     /// @returns The number of bytes occupied by data type.
     949      4072864 :     static size_t data_type_size(data_type adata_type) {
     950      4072864 :         return dnnl_data_type_size(convert_to_c(adata_type));
     951              :     }
     952              : 
     953              :     /// Memory format kind
     954              :     enum class format_kind {
     955              :         /// Undefined memory format kind, used for empty memory descriptors.
     956              :         undef = dnnl_format_kind_undef,
     957              :         /// A special format kind that indicates that the actual format will be
     958              :         /// selected by a primitive automatically.
     959              :         any = dnnl_format_kind_any,
     960              :         /// A tensor in a generic format described by the stride and blocking
     961              :         /// values in each dimension.
     962              :         blocked = dnnl_blocked,
     963              :         /// Format kind for sparse tensors.
     964              :         sparse = dnnl_format_kind_sparse,
     965              :         /// Format kind for host scalars.
     966              :         host_scalar = dnnl_format_kind_host_scalar,
     967              :         /// A special format kind that indicates that tensor format is opaque.
     968              :         opaque = dnnl_format_kind_opaque,
     969              :     };
     970              : 
     971              :     /// Sparse encodings.
     972              :     /// @sa @ref dev_guide_sparsity
     973              :     enum class sparse_encoding {
     974              :         /// Undefined sparse encoding kind, used for empty memory descriptors.
     975              :         undef = dnnl_sparse_encoding_undef,
     976              :         /// Compressed Sparse Row (CSR) encoding.
     977              :         csr = dnnl_csr,
     978              :         /// An encoding that is used for an opaque storage schema for
     979              :         /// tensors with unstructured sparsity. A memory descriptor with the
     980              :         /// packed encoding cannot be used to create a memory object. It can
     981              :         /// only be used to create a primitive descriptor to query the
     982              :         /// actual memory descriptor (similar to the format tag `any`).
     983              :         packed = dnnl_packed,
     984              :         /// Coordinate Sparse (COO) encoding.
     985              :         coo = dnnl_coo,
     986              : #if DNNL_EXPERIMENTAL_GROUPED_MEMORY
     987              :         /// Grouped Encoding.
     988              :         grouped = dnnl_grouped,
     989              : #endif
     990              :     };
     991              : 
     992              :     /// Memory format tag specification.
     993              :     ///
     994              :     /// Memory format tags can be further divided into two categories:
     995              :     ///
     996              :     ///  - Domain-agnostic names, i.e. names that do not depend on the tensor
     997              :     ///    usage in the specific primitive. These names use letters from `a`
     998              :     ///    to `f` to denote logical dimensions and form the order in which the
     999              :     ///    dimensions are laid in memory. For example,
    1000              :     ///    #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ab is used to denote a 2D tensor where the
    1001              :     ///    second logical dimension (denoted as `b`) is the innermost, i.e.
    1002              :     ///    has stride = 1, and the first logical dimension (`a`) is laid out in
    1003              :     ///    memory with stride equal to the size of the second dimension. On the
    1004              :     ///    other hand, #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ba is the transposed version
    1005              :     ///    of the same tensor: the outermost dimension (`a`) becomes the
    1006              :     ///    innermost one.
    1007              :     ///
    1008              :     ///  - Domain-specific names, i.e. names that make sense only in the
    1009              :     ///    context of a certain domain, such as CNN. These names are
    1010              :     ///    aliases to the corresponding domain-agnostic tags and used mostly
    1011              :     ///    for convenience. For example, #dnnl::memory::format_tag::nc
    1012              :     ///    is used to denote 2D CNN activations tensor memory format, where
    1013              :     ///    the channels dimension is the innermost one and the batch dimension
    1014              :     ///    is the outermost one. Moreover, #dnnl::memory::format_tag::nc is
    1015              :     ///    an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ab, because for
    1016              :     ///    CNN primitives the logical dimensions of activations tensors come
    1017              :     ///    in order: batch, channels, spatial.  In other words, batch
    1018              :     ///    corresponds to the first logical dimension (`a`), and channels
    1019              :     ///    correspond to the second one (`b`).
    1020              :     ///
    1021              :     /// The following domain-specific notation applies to memory format tags:
    1022              :     ///  - @c 'n' denotes the mini-batch dimension
    1023              :     ///  - @c 'c' denotes a channels dimension
    1024              :     ///  - When there are multiple channel dimensions (for example,
    1025              :     ///    in convolution weights tensor), @c 'i' and @c 'o' denote dimensions
    1026              :     ///    of input and output channels
    1027              :     ///  - @c 'g' denotes a groups dimension for convolution weights
    1028              :     ///  - @c 'd', @c 'h', and @c 'w' denote spatial depth, height, and width
    1029              :     ///    respectively
    1030              :     ///
    1031              :     /// See @ref dnnl_format_tag_t for a detailed description.
    1032              :     enum class format_tag {
    1033              :         /// Undefined memory format tag
    1034              :         undef = dnnl_format_tag_undef,
    1035              :         /// Placeholder memory format tag. Used to instruct the primitive to
    1036              :         /// select a format automatically.
    1037              :         any = dnnl_format_tag_any,
    1038              : 
    1039              :         /// plain 1D tensor
    1040              :         a = dnnl_a,
    1041              : 
    1042              :         /// plain 2D tensor
    1043              :         ab = dnnl_ab,
    1044              :         /// permuted 2D tensor
    1045              :         ba = dnnl_ba,
    1046              : 
    1047              :         /// plain 3D tensor
    1048              :         abc = dnnl_abc,
    1049              :         /// permuted 3D tensor
    1050              :         acb = dnnl_acb,
    1051              :         /// permuted 3D tensor
    1052              :         bac = dnnl_bac,
    1053              :         /// permuted 3D tensor
    1054              :         bca = dnnl_bca,
    1055              :         /// permuted 3D tensor
    1056              :         cba = dnnl_cba,
    1057              : 
    1058              :         /// plain 4D tensor
    1059              :         abcd = dnnl_abcd,
    1060              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1061              :         abdc = dnnl_abdc,
    1062              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1063              :         acbd = dnnl_acbd,
    1064              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1065              :         acdb = dnnl_acdb,
    1066              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1067              :         adbc = dnnl_adbc,
    1068              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1069              :         bacd = dnnl_bacd,
    1070              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1071              :         bcda = dnnl_bcda,
    1072              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1073              :         cdba = dnnl_cdba,
    1074              :         /// permuted 4D tensor
    1075              :         dcab = dnnl_dcab,
    1076              : 
    1077              :         /// plain 5D tensor
    1078              :         abcde = dnnl_abcde,
    1079              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1080              :         abdec = dnnl_abdec,
    1081              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1082              :         acbde = dnnl_acbde,
    1083              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1084              :         acdeb = dnnl_acdeb,
    1085              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1086              :         bacde = dnnl_bacde,
    1087              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1088              :         bcdea = dnnl_bcdea,
    1089              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1090              :         cdeba = dnnl_cdeba,
    1091              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1092              :         decab = dnnl_decab,
    1093              :         /// permuted 5D tensor
    1094              :         abced = dnnl_abced,
    1095              : 
    1096              :         /// plain 6D tensor
    1097              :         abcdef = dnnl_abcdef,
    1098              :         /// permuted 6D tensor
    1099              :         abdfce = dnnl_abdfce,
    1100              :         /// permuted 6D tensor
    1101              :         acbdef = dnnl_acbdef,
    1102              :         /// permuted 6D tensor
    1103              :         abdefc = dnnl_abdefc,
    1104              :         /// permuted 6D tensor
    1105              :         defcab = dnnl_defcab,
    1106              :         /// permuted 6D tensor
    1107              :         abcdfe = dnnl_abcdfe,
    1108              : 
    1109              :         /// plain 7D tensor
    1110              :         abcdefg = dnnl_abcdefg,
    1111              :         /// permuted 7D tensor
    1112              :         abcdegf = dnnl_abcdegf,
    1113              : 
    1114              :         /// plain 8D tensor
    1115              :         abcdefgh = dnnl_abcdefgh,
    1116              :         /// permuted 8D tensor
    1117              :         abcdefhg = dnnl_abcdefhg,
    1118              : 
    1119              :         /// plain 9D tensor
    1120              :         abcdefghi = dnnl_abcdefghi,
    1121              :         /// permuted 9D tensor
    1122              :         abcdefgih = dnnl_abcdefgih,
    1123              : 
    1124              :         /// plain 10D tensor
    1125              :         abcdefghij = dnnl_abcdefghij,
    1126              :         /// permuted 10D tensor
    1127              :         abcdefghji = dnnl_abcdefghji,
    1128              : 
    1129              :         /// plain 11D tensor
    1130              :         abcdefghijk = dnnl_abcdefghijk,
    1131              :         /// permuted 11D tensor
    1132              :         abcdefghikj = dnnl_abcdefghikj,
    1133              : 
    1134              :         /// plain 12D tensor
    1135              :         abcdefghijkl = dnnl_abcdefghijkl,
    1136              :         /// permuted 12D tensor
    1137              :         abcdefghijlk = dnnl_abcdefghijlk,
    1138              : 
    1139              :         /// 1D tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::a
    1140              :         x = a,
    1141              :         /// 2D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ab
    1142              :         nc = ab,
    1143              :         /// 2D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ba
    1144              :         cn = ba,
    1145              :         /// 2D RNN statistics tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ab
    1146              :         tn = ab,
    1147              :         /// 2D RNN statistics tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ba
    1148              :         nt = ba,
    1149              :         /// 3D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abc
    1150              :         ncw = abc,
    1151              :         /// 3D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acb
    1152              :         nwc = acb,
    1153              :         /// 4D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcd
    1154              :         nchw = abcd,
    1155              :         /// 4D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acdb
    1156              :         nhwc = acdb,
    1157              :         /// 4D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bcda
    1158              :         chwn = bcda,
    1159              :         /// 5D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcde
    1160              :         ncdhw = abcde,
    1161              :         /// 5D CNN activations tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acdeb
    1162              :         ndhwc = acdeb,
    1163              : 
    1164              :         /// 2D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ab
    1165              :         oi = ab,
    1166              :         /// 2D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::ba
    1167              :         io = ba,
    1168              :         /// 3D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abc
    1169              :         oiw = abc,
    1170              :         /// 3D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acb
    1171              :         owi = acb,
    1172              :         /// 3D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::cba
    1173              :         wio = cba,
    1174              :         /// 3D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bca
    1175              :         iwo = bca,
    1176              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcd
    1177              :         oihw = abcd,
    1178              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::cdba
    1179              :         hwio = cdba,
    1180              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acdb
    1181              :         ohwi = acdb,
    1182              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bcda
    1183              :         ihwo = bcda,
    1184              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bacd
    1185              :         iohw = bacd,
    1186              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcde
    1187              :         oidhw = abcde,
    1188              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::cdeba
    1189              :         dhwio = cdeba,
    1190              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acdeb
    1191              :         odhwi = acdeb,
    1192              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bacde
    1193              :         iodhw = bacde,
    1194              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bcdea
    1195              :         idhwo = bcdea,
    1196              : 
    1197              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcd
    1198              :         goiw = abcd,
    1199              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abdc
    1200              :         gowi = abdc,
    1201              :         /// 4D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::dcab
    1202              :         wigo = dcab,
    1203              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abdec
    1204              :         gohwi = abdec,
    1205              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcde
    1206              :         goihw = abcde,
    1207              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::decab
    1208              :         hwigo = decab,
    1209              :         /// 5D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::acbde
    1210              :         giohw = acbde,
    1211              :         /// 6D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcdef
    1212              :         goidhw = abcdef,
    1213              :         /// 6D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcdef
    1214              :         giodhw = acbdef,
    1215              :         /// 6D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abdefc
    1216              :         godhwi = abdefc,
    1217              :         /// 6D CNN weights tensor with groups; an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::defcab
    1218              :         dhwigo = defcab,
    1219              : 
    1220              :         /// 3D RNN data tensor in the format (seq_length, batch, input
    1221              :         /// channels); an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abc.
    1222              :         tnc = abc,
    1223              :         /// 3D RNN data tensor in the format (batch, seq_length, input
    1224              :         /// channels); an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::bac.
    1225              :         ntc = bac,
    1226              :         /// 4D RNN states tensor in the format (num_layers, num_directions,
    1227              :         /// batch, state channels); an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcd.
    1228              :         ldnc = abcd,
    1229              :         /// 5D RNN weights tensor in the format (num_layers, num_directions,
    1230              :         /// input_channels, num_gates, output_channels);
    1231              :         /// an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcde.
    1232              :         ///
    1233              :         ///  - For LSTM cells, the gates order is input, forget, candidate
    1234              :         ///    and output gate.
    1235              :         ///  - For GRU cells, the gates order is update, reset and output gate.
    1236              :         ldigo = abcde,
    1237              :         /// 5D RNN weights tensor in the format (num_layers, num_directions,
    1238              :         /// num_gates, output_channels, input_channels);
    1239              :         /// an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abdec.
    1240              :         ///
    1241              :         ///  - For LSTM cells, the gates order is input, forget, candidate
    1242              :         ///    and output gate.
    1243              :         ///  - For GRU cells, the gates order is update, reset and output gate.
    1244              :         ldgoi = abdec,
    1245              :         /// 4D LSTM projection tensor in the format (num_layers, num_directions,
    1246              :         /// num_channels_in_hidden_state, num_channels_in_recurrent_projection);
    1247              :         /// an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcd.
    1248              :         ldio = abcd,
    1249              :         /// 4D LSTM projection tensor in the format (num_layers, num_directions,
    1250              :         /// num_channels_in_recurrent_projection, num_channels_in_hidden_state);
    1251              :         /// an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abdc.
    1252              :         ldoi = abdc,
    1253              :         /// 4D RNN bias tensor in the format (num_layers, num_directions,
    1254              :         /// num_gates, output_channels);
    1255              :         /// an alias for #dnnl::memory::format_tag::abcd.
    1256              :         ///
    1257              :         ///  - For LSTM cells, the gates order is input, forget, candidate
    1258              :         ///    and output gate.
    1259              :         ///  - For GRU cells, the gates order is update, reset and output gate.
    1260              :         ldgo = abcd,
    1261              :

    1263              : 
    1264              :         AB16b16a = dnnl_AB16b16a,

    2914              :         /// Function for creating a memory descriptor for CSR sparse encoding.
    2915              :         ///
    2916              :         /// The created memory descriptor will describe a memory object that

    2920              :         ///  - 1: indices
    2921              :         ///  - 2: pointers

    2923              :         /// @param adims Tensor dimensions.

    2949              :         /// Function for creating a memory descriptor for COO sparse encodings.
    2950              :         ///
    2951              :         /// The created memory descriptor will describe a memory object that

    2953              :         /// The buffers have the following meaning and assigned numbers (index):
    2954              :         ///  - 0: values
    2955              :         ///  - 1: indices for dimension 0
    2956              :         ///  - 2: indices for dimension 1 ...
    2957              :         ///  - n: indices for dimension n-1
    2958              :         ///
    2959              :         /// @param adims Tensor dimensions.
    2960              :         /// @param adata_type Data precision/type.

    2962              :         /// @param index_dt Data type of indices.

    2982              :         /// Function for creating a memory descriptor for packed sparse

    2990              :         /// @warning

    3017              : #if DNNL_EXPERIMENTAL_GROUPED_MEMORY
    3018              :         /// Creates a memory descriptor for grouped encoding, that
    3019              :         /// stores multiple independent sub-buffers (groups) with one
    3020              :         /// dimension varying in size.
    3021              :         ///
    3022              :         /// Common use case is Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) workloads where each expert
    3023              :         /// processes different numbers of tokens.
    3024              :         ///
    3025              :         /// Memory layout consists of two buffers:
    3026              :         /// - Buffer 0 (values): Concatenated data [group0 | group1 | ... | groupN-1]
    3027              :         /// - Buffer 1 (offsets): Cumulative indices [size0, size0 + size1, ...]
    3028              :         ///
    3029              :         /// Example in the context of MoE:
    3030              :         /// Below is how to describe 3 experts with token counts [1, 3, 5] and feature dim 256
    3031              :         /// - Descriptor dims: {9, 256} where 9 is 1 + 3 + 5 (total tokens)
    3032              :         /// - Variable dimension: 0 (token count varies per expert)
    3033              :         /// - Group count: 3 (number of experts)
    3034              :         /// - Values buffer: 9 x 256 elements
    3035              :         /// - Offsets buffer: [1, 4, 9] (cumulative token counts)
    3036              :         ///
    3037              :         /// @note
    3038              :         ///     Only s32 offsets are currently supported.
    3039              :         ///
    3040              :         /// @param adims Tensor dimensions. For the variable dimension,
    3041              :         ///     specify the total size (sum across all groups).
    3042              :         /// @param adata_type Data type of tensor elements.
    3043              :         /// @param variable_dim_idx Index of dimension that varies between groups.
    3044              :         /// @param group_count Number of groups (e.g., number of experts in MoE).
    3045              :         /// @param offsets_dt Data type of offsets buffer. Default: s32.
    3046              :         /// @param allow_empty Allow construction to fail without exception.
    3047              :         ///     Returns zero descriptor on failure. Default: false.
    3048              :         /// @returns Memory descriptor for grouped encoding.
    3049          888 :         static desc grouped(const dims &adims, data_type adata_type,
    3050              :                 int variable_dim_idx, dim group_count,
    3051              :                 data_type offsets_dt = data_type::s32,
    3052              :                 bool allow_empty = false) {
    3053              : 
    3054          888 :             validate_dims(adims);
    3055          888 :             dnnl_memory_desc_t md = nullptr;
    3056          888 :             dnnl_status_t status
    3057         1776 :                     = dnnl_memory_desc_create_with_grouped_encoding(&md,
    3058          888 :                             (int)adims.size(), adims.data(),
    3059              :                             convert_to_c(adata_type), variable_dim_idx,
    3060              :                             group_count, convert_to_c(offsets_dt));
    3061          888 :             if (!allow_empty)
    3062          888 :                 error::wrap_c_api(
    3063              :                         status, "could not create a grouped memory descriptor");
    3064         1196 :             return desc {md};
    3065              :         }
    3066              : #endif

    3121              : 
    3122              :         /// Constructs a memory descriptor by reshaping an existing one. The
    3123              :         /// new memory descriptor inherits the data type. This operation is
    3124              :         /// valid only for memory descriptors that have format_kind set to
    3125              :         /// #dnnl::memory::format_kind::blocked or
    3126              :         /// #dnnl::memory::format_kind::any.
    3127              :         ///
    3128              :         /// The operation ensures that the transformation of the physical memory
    3129              :         /// format corresponds to the transformation of the logical dimensions.
    3130              :         /// If such transformation is impossible, the function either throws an
    3131              :         /// exception (default) or returns a zero memory descriptor depending on
    3132              :         /// the `allow_empty` flag.
    3133              :         ///
    3134              :         /// The reshape operation can be described as a combination of the
    3135              :         /// following basic operations:
    3136              :         /// 1. Add a dimension of size `1`. This is always possible.
    3137              :         /// 2. Remove a dimension of size `1`. This is possible only if the
    3138              :         ///    dimension has no padding (i.e.
    3139              :         ///    `padded_dims[dim] == dims[dim] && dims[dim] == 1`).
    3140              :         /// 3. Split a dimension into multiple ones. This is possible only if
    3141              :         ///    the product of all tensor dimensions stays constant and the
    3142              :         ///    dimension being split does not have padding (i.e.
    3143              :         ///    `padded_dims[dim] = dims[dim]`).
    3144              :         /// 4. Join multiple consecutive dimensions into a single one. As in
    3145              :         ///    the cases above, this requires that the dimensions do not have
    3146              :         ///    padding and that the memory format is such that in physical
    3147              :         ///    memory these dimensions are dense and have the same order as
    3148              :         ///    their logical counterparts. This also assumes that these
    3149              :         ///    dimensions are not blocked.
    3150              :         ///    - Here, 'dense' means:
    3151              :         ///      `stride for dim[i] == (stride for dim[i + 1]) * dim[i + 1]`;
    3152              :         ///    - And 'same order' means:
    3153              :         ///      `i < j` if and only if `stride for dim[j] <= stride for dim[i]`.
    3154              :         ///
    3155              :         /// @warning
    3156              :         ///     Some combinations of physical memory layout and/or offsets or
    3157              :         ///     dimensions may result in a failure to make a reshape.
    3158              :         ///
    3159              :         /// @param adims New dimensions. The product of dimensions must
    3160              :         ///     remain constant.
    3161              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    3162              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case a
    3163              :         ///     zero memory descriptor will be returned. This flag is optional
    3164              :         ///     and defaults to false.
    3165              :         /// @returns A new memory descriptor with new dimensions.

    3171        49789 :             if (!allow_empty)
    3172        27800 :                 error::wrap_c_api(
    3173              :                         status, "could not reshape a memory descriptor");
    3174        97778 :             return desc(out_md);
    3175              :         }

    3177              :         /// Constructs a memory descriptor by permuting axes in an existing
    3178              :         /// one.
    3179              :         ///
    3180              :         /// The physical memory layout representation is adjusted accordingly
    3181              :         /// to maintain the consistency between the logical and physical parts
    3182              :         /// of the memory descriptor. The new memory descriptor inherits the
    3183              :         /// data type.
    3184              :         ///
    3185              :         /// The new memory descriptor inherits the data type. This operation is
    3186              :         /// valid only for memory descriptors that have format_kind set to
    3187              :         /// #dnnl::memory::format_kind::blocked or
    3188              :         /// #dnnl::memory::format_kind::any.
    3189              :         ///
    3190              :         /// The logical axes will be permuted in the following manner:
    3191              :         /// @code
    3192              :         /// for (i = 0; i < get_ndims(); i++)
    3193              :         ///     new_desc.dims()[permutation[i]] = dims()[i];
    3194              :         /// @endcode
    3195              :         ///
    3196              :         /// Example:
    3197              :         /// @code
    3198              :         ///     std::vector<int> permutation = {1, 0}; // swap the first and
    3199              :         ///                                            // the second axes
    3200              :         ///     dnnl::memory::desc in_md(
    3201              :         ///             {2, 3}, data_type, memory::format_tag::ab);
    3202              :         ///     dnnl::memory::desc expect_out_md(
    3203              :         ///             {3, 2}, data_type, memory::format_tag::ba);
    3204              :         ///
    3205              :         ///     assert(in_md.permute_axes(permutation) == expect_out_md);
    3206              :         /// @endcode
    3207              :         ///
    3208              :         /// @param permutation Axes permutation.
    3209              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    3210              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case a
    3211              :         ///     zero memory descriptor will be returned. This flag is optional
    3212              :         ///     and defaults to false.
    3213              :         /// @returns A new memory descriptor with new dimensions.
    3214        31040 :         desc permute_axes(const std::vector<int> &permutation,
    3215              :                 bool allow_empty = false) const {
    3216        31040 :             validate_dims(permutation, get_ndims());
    3217        31040 :             dnnl_memory_desc_t out_md = nullptr;

    3220        31040 :             if (!allow_empty)
    3221        31040 :                 error::wrap_c_api(status,
    3222              :                         "could not permute axes of a memory descriptor");
    3223        61052 :             return desc(out_md);
    3224              :         }

    3444              :     /// Constructs a memory object.

    3447              :     /// object will have the underlying buffer set. In this case, the buffer
    3448              :     /// will be initialized as if #dnnl::memory::set_data_handle() had been

    3451              :     /// @sa memory::set_data_handle()

    3457              :     ///       doesn't own the buffer.
    3458              :     ///     - The #DNNL_MEMORY_ALLOCATE special value. Instructs the library to
    3459              :     ///       allocate the buffer for the memory object. In this case the

    3461              :     ///     - #DNNL_MEMORY_NONE to create dnnl::memory without an underlying

    3470              :     /// will be initialized as if #dnnl::memory::set_data_handle() had been

    3473              :     /// @sa memory::set_data_handle()

    3480              :     ///       doesn't own the buffer.
    3481              :     ///     - The #DNNL_MEMORY_ALLOCATE special value. Instructs the library to
    3482              :     ///       allocate the buffer for the memory object. In this case the
    3483              :     ///       library owns the buffer.
    3484              :     ///     - #DNNL_MEMORY_NONE Instructs the library to skip allocation of the

    3513              :     /// Constructs a memory object that wraps a host-side scalar value.
    3514              :     ///
    3515              :     /// @note The scalar value is copied into the newly allocated memory storage,
    3516              :     ///     so the user does not need to manage the lifetime of the original scalar data.
    3517              :     ///

    3570              :     /// Sets an underlying memory buffer that corresponds to the given index.

    3625              :     /// Maps a memory object and returns a host-side pointer to a memory

    3632              :     /// Mapping is an exclusive operation - a memory object cannot be used in
    3633              :     /// other operations until it is unmapped via #dnnl::memory::unmap_data()
    3634              :     /// call.
    3635              :     ///

    3639              :     ///     corresponding execution stream.

    3641              :     /// @note

    3726              : /// Post-ops.

    3763              : 
    3764              :     /// Appends an accumulation (sum) post-op. Prior to accumulating the
    3765              :     /// result, the previous value will be will be reduced by zero point
    3766              :     /// @p zero_point and multiplied by a scaling factor @p scale.
    3767              :     ///
    3768              :     /// The kind of this post-op is #dnnl::primitive::kind::sum.
    3769              :     ///
    3770              :     /// This feature may improve performance for cases like dequantize the
    3771              :     /// asymmetrically quantized sum's src1 tensor to f32 domain before
    3772              :     /// performing the sum operation by subtracting @p zero_point before the
    3773              :     /// scaling.
    3774              :     ///
    3775              :     /// In the simplest case when the accumulation is the only post-op,
    3776              :     /// the computations will be `dst[:] := scale * (dst[:] - zero_point) +
    3777              :     /// op(...)` instead of `dst[:] := op(...)`.
    3778              :     ///
    3779              :     /// If @p data_type is specified, the original dst tensor will be
    3780              :     /// reinterpreted as a tensor with the provided data type. Because it is a
    3781              :     /// reinterpretation, data_type and dst data type should have the same size.
    3782              :     /// As a result, computations will be `dst[:] <- scale *
    3783              :     /// (as_data_type(dst[:]) - zero_point) + op(...)` instead of
    3784              :     /// `dst[:] <- op(...)`.
    3785              :     ///
    3786              :     /// @note
    3787              :     ///     This post-op executes in-place and does not change the
    3788              :     ///     destination layout.
    3789              :     ///
    3790              :     /// @param scale Scaling factor.
    3791              :     /// @param zero_point Zero point.
    3792              :     /// @param data_type Data type.
    3793       173611 :     void append_sum(float scale = 1.f, int32_t zero_point = 0,
    3794              :             memory::data_type data_type = memory::data_type::undef) {
    3795       173611 :         error::wrap_c_api(dnnl_post_ops_append_sum(get(), scale, zero_point,
    3796              :                                   memory::convert_to_c(data_type)),
    3797              :                 "could not append a sum post-op");
    3798       173582 :     }
    3799              :

    3872              :     /// Appends a depthwise post-op convolution.

    3874              :     /// This post-op can only be fused with a 2D 1x1 convolution (convolution

    3941              :     /// Appends a binary post-op.
    3942              :     ///
