    4032              : 
    4033              :     /// Appends a prelu forward post-op.
    4034              :     ///
    4035              :     /// The kind of this post-op is #dnnl::primitive::kind::prelu.
    4036              :     ///
    4037              :     /// The post-op can be defined as:
    4038              :     ///
    4039              :     ///      dst[:] <- prelu(dst[:], weights[:])
    4040              :     ///      prelu:
    4041              :     ///      dst[:] <- dst[:] if dst[:] > 0
    4042              :     ///      dst[:] <- dst[:] * weights[:] if dst[:] <= 0
    4043              :     ///
    4044              :     ///
    4045              :     /// Example usage:
    4046              :     /// @code
    4047              :     ///     int mb = 32, oc = 32,
    4048              :     ///         oh = 14, ow = 14; // convolution output params
    4049              :     ///     // unique weights per output channel
    4050              :     ///     vector<float> weights = { ... };
    4051              :     ///     int oc_dim = 1; // mb_dim = 0, channel_dim = 1, height_dim = 2, ...
    4052              :     ///
    4053              :     ///     // construct a convolution descriptor
    4054              :     ///     dnnl::convolution::desc conv_d;
    4055              :     ///
    4056              :     ///     dnnl::primitive_attr attr;
    4057              :     ///     attr.append_prelu(1 << oc_dim);
    4058              :     ///
    4059              :     ///     dnnl::primitive_desc conv_pd(conv_d, attr, engine);
    4060              :     ///     memory prelu_weights({{1}, dt::f32, {1}}, eng, weights.data());
    4061              :     ///
    4062              :     ///     std::unordered_map<int, memory> conv_args;
    4063              :     ///
    4064              :     ///     conv_args.insert(
    4065              :     ///      {DNNL_ARG_ATTR_MULTIPLE_POST_OP(0) | DNNL_ARG_WEIGHTS, prelu_weights})
    4066              :     /// @endcode
    4067              :     ///
    4068              :     /// @note
    4069              :     ///     The order of dimensions does not depend on how elements are laid
    4070              :     ///     out in memory. For example:
    4071              :     ///     - for a 2D CNN activations tensor the order is always (n, c)
    4072              :     ///     - for a 4D CNN activations tensor the order is always (n, c, h, w)
    4073              :     ///     - for a 5D CNN weights tensor the order is always
    4074              :     ///        (g, oc, ic, kh, kw)
    4075              :     ///
    4076              :     ///    Prelu weights tensor is passed in runtime execution phase. Prelu
    4077              :     ///    weights tensor data type is implicitly assumed as f32 using plain
    4078              :     ///    layout (a, ab, acb, acdb, acdeb).
    4079              :     ///
    4080              :     /// @param mask Defines the correspondence between the output tensor
    4081              :     ///     dimensions and the prelu weights tensor. The set i-th bit indicates
    4082              :     ///     that a dedicated weights value is used for each index along that
    4083              :     ///     dimension. Set the mask to 0 to use a common weights value
    4084              :     ///     for the whole output tensor.
    4085       132170 :     void append_prelu(int mask) {
    4086       132170 :         error::wrap_c_api(dnnl_post_ops_append_prelu(get(), mask),
    4087              :                 "could not append a prelu post-op");
    4088       132141 :     }
    4089              : 
    4090              :     /// Returns the parameters of a prelu post-op.
    4091              :     ///
    4092              :     /// @param index Index of the prelu post-op.
    4093              :     /// @param mask Weights mask of prelu post-op.
    4094           29 :     void get_params_prelu(int index, int &mask) const {
    4095           29 :         error::wrap_c_api(dnnl_post_ops_get_params_prelu(get(), index, &mask),
    4096              :                 "could not get parameters of a binary post-op");
    4097           29 :     }
    4098              : };

    4401              : 
    4402              :     /// Sets zero points for primitive operations for a given memory argument.
    4403              :     /// The zero points must be passed at execution time as an argument with
    4404              :     /// index #DNNL_ARG_ATTR_ZERO_POINTS | arg.
    4405              :     ///
    4406              :     /// @note If `is_on_host` is true, sets a single host-side zero point
    4407              :     /// for the specified memory argument. The zero point should be
    4408              :     /// passed as a host scalar memory object at execution time with index
    4409              :     /// #DNNL_ARG_ATTR_ZERO_POINTS | arg.
    4410              :     ///
    4411              :     /// @sa dnnl_primitive_attr_set_zero_points
    4412              :     ///
    4413              :     /// @param arg Parameter argument index as passed to the
    4414              :     ///     primitive::execute() call.
    4415              :     /// @param mask Zero point correspondence mask that defines the
    4416              :     ///     correspondence between the tensor dimensions and the zero points
    4417              :     ///     vector. The set i-th bit indicates that a dedicated zero point is

    4513              :     /// Sets quantization scale and shift parameters for RNN data tensors.
    4514              :     ///
    4515              :     /// For performance reasons, the low-precision configuration of the RNN
    4516              :     /// primitives expect input activations to have the unsigned 8-bit integer
    4517              :     /// data type. The scale and shift parameters are used to quantize
    4518              :     /// floating-point data to unsigned integer and must be passed to the RNN
    4519              :     /// primitive using attributes.
    4520              :     ///
    4521              :     /// The quantization formula is `scale * data + shift`.
    4522              :     ///
    4523              :     /// Example usage:
    4524              :     /// @code
    4525              :     ///     // RNN parameters
    4526              :     ///     int l = 2, t = 2, mb = 32, sic = 32, slc = 32, dic = 32, dlc = 32;
    4527              :     ///     // Activations quantization parameters
    4528              :     ///     float scale = 63.f, shift = 64.f;
    4529              :     ///
    4530              :     ///     primitive_attr attr;
    4531              :     ///
    4532              :     ///     // Set scale and shift for int8 quantization of activation
    4533              :     ///     attr.set_rnn_data_qparams(scale, shift);
    4534              :     ///
    4535              :     ///     // Create an RNN primitive descriptor.
    4536              :     ///     vanilla_rnn_forward::primitive_desc rnn_d(
    4537              :     ///             engine, /* arguments */, attr);
    4538              :     /// @endcode
    4539              :     ///
    4540              :     /// @note
    4541              :     ///     Quantization scale and shift are common for src_layer, src_iter,
    4542              :     ///     dst_iter, and dst_layer.
    4543              :     ///
    4544              :     /// @param scale The value to scale the data by.
    4545              :     /// @param shift The value to shift the data by.
    4546          299 :     void set_rnn_data_qparams(float scale, float shift) {
    4547          299 :         error::wrap_c_api(
    4548              :                 dnnl_primitive_attr_set_rnn_data_qparams(get(), scale, shift),
    4549              :                 "could not set RNN data quantization parameters primitive "
    4550              :                 "attribute");
    4551          299 :     }
    4552              : 
    4553              :     /// Returns the quantization scale and shift parameters for RNN data

    4559              :     ///

    4572              :     /// Sets quantization scaling factors for RNN weights tensors. The
    4573              :     /// low-precision configuration of the RNN primitives expect input weights
    4574              :     /// to use the signed 8-bit integer data type. The scaling factors are
    4575              :     /// used to quantize floating-point data to signed integer and must be
    4576              :     /// passed to RNN primitives using attributes.
    4577              :     ///
    4578              :     /// @note
    4579              :     ///     The dimension order is always native and does not depend on the
    4580              :     ///     actual layout used. For example, five-dimensional weights always
    4581              :     ///     have (l, d, i, g, o) logical dimension ordering.
    4582              :     ///
    4583              :     /// @note
    4584              :     ///     Quantization scales are common for weights_layer and
    4585              :     ///     weights_iteration
    4586              :     ///
    4587              :     /// @param mask Scaling factors correspondence mask that defines the
    4588              :     ///     correspondence between the output tensor dimensions and the @p
    4589              :     ///     scales vector. The set i-th bit indicates that a dedicated scaling
    4590              :     ///     factor should be used each index along that dimension. Set the
    4591              :     ///     mask to 0 to use a common scaling factor for the whole output
    4592              :     ///     tensor.
    4593              :     /// @param scales Constant vector of output scaling factors. The following
    4594              :     ///     equality must hold:
    4595              :     ///     \f$scales.size() = \prod\limits_{d \in mask} weights.dims[d].\f$
    4596              :     ///     Violations can only be detected when the attributes are used to
    4597              :     ///     create a primitive descriptor.

    5700              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_primitives_common
    5701              : /// @{
    5702              : 
    5703              : /// A base class for descriptors of all primitives that support iteration
    5704              : ///     over multiple implementations.
    5705     22306867 : struct primitive_desc : public primitive_desc_base {
    5706       270651 :     using primitive_desc_base::primitive_desc_base;
    5707              : 
    5708     10726280 :     primitive_desc() = default;
    5709              : 
    5710              :     /// Changes the primitive descriptor to point to the next available
    5711              :     /// implementation.
    5712              :     ///
    5713              :     /// @returns @c true on success and @c false if the last available
    5714              :     /// implementation has already been reached. In the latter case, the
    5715              :     /// primitive descriptor itself is kept unchanged.
    5716         2392 :     bool next_impl() {
    5717         2392 :         dnnl_status_t status = dnnl_primitive_desc_next_impl(get());
    5718         2392 :         if (status == dnnl_last_impl_reached) return false;
    5719         2298 :         error::wrap_c_api(status, "last available implementation is reached");
    5720         2298 :         return true;
    5721              :     }
    5722              : };
    5723              : 
    5724              : /// @} dnnl_api_primitives_common
    5725              : 
    5726              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_convolution Convolution
    5727              : ///
    5728              : /// A primitive to perform 1D, 2D or 3D convolution. Supported variants are
    5729              : /// forward propagation, backward propagation, and weights gradient with or
    5730              : /// without bias.
    5731              : ///
    5732              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_convolution in developer guide
    5733              : ///
    5734              : /// @{
    5735              : 
    5736              : /// Convolution forward propagation primitive.
    5737        37826 : struct convolution_forward : public primitive {
    5738              :     /// Primitive descriptor for a convolution forward propagation primitive.
    5739        63979 :     struct primitive_desc : public dnnl::primitive_desc {
    5740              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    5741        18136 :         primitive_desc() = default;
    5742              : 
    5743              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a convolution forward
    5744              :         ///     propagation primitive with bias.
    5745              :         ///
    5746              :         /// @note
    5747              :         ///     All the memory descriptors may be initialized with the
    5748              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
    5749              :         ///
    5750              :         /// Arrays @p strides, @p padding_l, and @p padding_r contain values

    6039              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a convolution backward
    6040              :         ///     propagation primitive.
    6041              :         ///
    6042              :         /// @note
    6043              :         ///     All the memory descriptors may be initialized with the
    6044              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
    6045              :         ///
    6046              :         /// Arrays @p strides, @p padding_l, and @p padding_r contain values
    6047              :         /// for spatial dimensions only and hence must have the same number of
    6048              :         /// elements as there are spatial dimensions. The order of values is
    6049              :         /// the same as in the tensor: depth (for 3D tensors), height (for 3D
    6050              :         /// and 2D tensors), and width.

    6068              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional

    6233              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a convolution weights gradient

    6235              :         ///
    6236              :         /// @note

    6238              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
    6239              :         ///
    6240              :         /// Arrays @p strides, @p padding_l, and @p padding_r contain values
    6241              :         /// for spatial dimensions only and hence must have the same number of
    6242              :         /// elements as there are spatial dimensions. The order of values is
    6243              :         /// the same as in the tensor: depth (for 3D tensors), height (for 3D
    6244              :         /// and 2D tensors), and width.
    6245              :         ///
    6246              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
    6247              :         /// @param aalgorithm Convolution algorithm. Possible values are

    6255              :         /// @param diff_dst_desc Diff destination memory descriptor.
    6256              :         /// @param strides Strides for each spatial dimension.
    6257              :         /// @param padding_l Vector of padding values for low indices for each

    6259              :         /// @param padding_r Vector of padding values for high indices for
    6260              :         ///     each spatial dimension `([[back,] bottom,] right)`.

    6533              : /// @} dnnl_api_convolution
    6534              : //
    6535              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_deconvolution Deconvolution
    6536              : ///
    6537              : /// A primitive to perform 1D, 2D or 3D deconvolution. Supported variants are
    6538              : /// forward propagation, backward propagation, and weights gradient with or
    6539              : /// without bias.
    6540              : ///
    6541              : /// @{
    6542              : 
    6543              : /// Deconvolution forward propagation primitive.
    6544          980 : struct deconvolution_forward : public primitive {
    6545              :     /// Primitive descriptor for a deconvolution forward propagation primitive.
    6546        11655 :     struct primitive_desc : public dnnl::primitive_desc {
    6547              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    6548         8174 :         primitive_desc() = default;
    6549              : 
    6550              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a deconvolution forward
    6551              :         ///     propagation primitive with bias.
    6552              :         ///
    6553              :         /// @note
    6554              :         ///     All the memory descriptors may be initialized with the
    6555              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
    6556              :         ///
    6557              :         /// Arrays @p strides, @p padding_l, and @p padding_r contain values
    6558              :         /// for spatial dimensions only and hence must have the same number of
    6559              :         /// elements as there are spatial dimensions. The order of values is
    6560              :         /// the same as in the tensor: depth (for 3D tensors), height (for 3D
    6561              :         /// and 2D tensors), and width.
    6562              :         ///
    6563              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.

    6839              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a deconvolution backward

    7033              :         ///
    7034              :         /// @note

    7036              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
    7037              :         ///
    7038              :         /// Arrays @p strides, @p padding_l, and @p padding_r contain values
    7039              :         /// for spatial dimensions only and hence must have the same number of
    7040              :         /// elements as there are spatial dimensions. The order of values is
    7041              :         /// the same as in the tensor: depth (for 3D tensors), height (for 3D
    7042              :         /// and 2D tensors), and width.
    7043              :         ///
    7044              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
    7045              :         /// @param aalgorithm Deconvolution algorithm. Possible values are

    7326              : 
    7327              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_lrn LRN
    7328              : ///
    7329              : /// A primitive to perform local response normalization (LRN) across or within
    7330              : /// channels.
    7331              : ///
    7332              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_lrn in developer guide
    7333              : ///
    7334              : /// @{
    7335              : 
    7336              : /// Local response normalization (LRN) forward propagation primitive.
    7337        32514 : struct lrn_forward : public primitive {
    7338              :     /// Primitive descriptor for an LRN forward propagation primitive.
    7339       140831 :     struct primitive_desc : public dnnl::primitive_desc {
    7340              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    7341        32596 :         primitive_desc() = default;
    7342              : 
    7343              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for an LRN forward propagation
    7344              :         ///     primitive.
    7345              :         ///
    7346              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
    7347              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
    7348              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
    7349              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
    7350              :         /// @param aalgorithm LRN algorithm kind: either
    7351              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::lrn_across_channels, or
    7352              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::lrn_within_channel.
    7353              :         /// @param src_desc Source memory descriptor.
    7354              :         /// @param dst_desc Destination memory descriptor.
    7355              :         /// @param local_size Regularization local size.
    7356              :         /// @param alpha The alpha regularization parameter.
    7357              :         /// @param beta The beta regularization parameter.
    7358              :         /// @param k The k regularization parameter.
    7359              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
    7360              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
    7361              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    7362              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
    7363              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
    7364              :         ///     defaults to false.
    7365        97607 :         primitive_desc(const engine &aengine, prop_kind aprop_kind,
    7366              :                 algorithm aalgorithm, const memory::desc &src_desc,
    7367              :                 const memory::desc &dst_desc, memory::dim local_size,

    7550              : 
    7551              : /// @} dnnl_api_lrn
    7552              : 
    7553              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_eltwise Eltwise
    7554              : ///
    7555              : /// A primitive to perform elementwise operations such as the
    7556              : /// rectifier linear unit (ReLU).
    7557              : ///
    7558              : /// Both forward and backward propagation primitives support in-place
    7559              : /// operation; that is, src and dst can refer to the same memory for forward
    7560              : /// propagation, and diff_dst and diff_src can refer to the same memory for
    7561              : /// backward propagation.
    7562              : ///
    7563              : /// @warning
    7564              : ///     Because the original source data is required for backward propagation,
    7565              : ///     in-place forward propagation is not generally supported in the
    7566              : ///     training mode. However, for algorithms supporting destination as input
    7567              : ///     memory, dst can be used for the backward propagation, which makes it
    7568              : ///     possible to get performance benefit even in the training mode.
    7569              : ///
    7570              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_eltwise in developer guide
    7571              : ///
    7572              : /// @{
    7573              : 
    7574              : /// Elementwise unary operation forward propagation primitive.
    7575       146328 : struct eltwise_forward : public primitive {
    7576              :     /// Primitive descriptor for an elementwise forward propagation primitive.
    7577       363087 :     struct primitive_desc : public dnnl::primitive_desc {
    7578              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    7579        63886 :         primitive_desc() = default;
    7580              : 
    7581              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for an elementwise forward
    7582              :         ///     propagation primitive.
    7583              :         ///
    7584              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
    7585              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
    7586              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
    7587              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
    7588              :         /// @param aalgorithm Elementwise algorithm kind.
    7589              :         /// @param src_desc Source memory descriptor.
    7590              :         /// @param dst_desc Destination memory descriptor.
    7591              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
    7592              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
    7593              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    7594              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
    7595              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
    7596              :         ///     defaults to false.
    7597              :         primitive_desc(const engine &aengine, prop_kind aprop_kind,
    7598              :                 algorithm aalgorithm, const memory::desc &src_desc,

    7601              :                 bool allow_empty = false)

    7609              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
    7610              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and

    7612              :         /// @param aalgorithm Elementwise algorithm kind.
    7613              :         /// @param src_desc Source memory descriptor.

    8105              : /// @} dnnl_api_softmax
    8106              : 
    8107              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_batch_normalization Batch Normalization
    8108              : ///
    8109              : /// A primitive to perform batch normalization.
    8110              : ///
    8111              : /// Both forward and backward propagation primitives support in-place
    8112              : /// operation; that is, src and dst can refer to the same memory for forward
    8113              : /// propagation, and diff_dst and diff_src can refer to the same memory for
    8114              : /// backward propagation.
    8115              : ///
    8116              : /// The batch normalization primitives computations can be controlled by
    8117              : /// specifying different @ref dnnl::normalization_flags values. For example,
    8118              : /// batch normalization forward propagation can be configured to either
    8119              : /// compute the mean and variance or take them as arguments. It can either
    8120              : /// perform scaling and shifting using gamma and beta parameters or not.
    8121              : /// Optionally, it can also perform a fused ReLU, which in case of training
    8122              : /// would also require a workspace.
    8123              : ///
    8124              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_batch_normalization in developer guide
    8125              : ///
    8126              : /// @{
    8127              : 
    8128              : /// Batch normalization forward propagation primitive.
    8129        29328 : struct batch_normalization_forward : public primitive {
    8130              :     /// Primitive descriptor for a batch normalization forward propagation
    8131              :     /// primitive.
    8132       146693 :     struct primitive_desc : public dnnl::primitive_desc {
    8133              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    8134        29419 :         primitive_desc() = default;
    8135              : 
    8136              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a batch normalization forward
    8137              :         /// propagation primitive.
    8138              :         ///
    8139              :         /// @note
    8140              :         ///     In-place operation is supported: the dst can refer to the same
    8141              :         ///     memory as the src.
    8142              :         ///
    8143              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
    8144              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
    8145              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training and
    8146              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
    8147              :         /// @param src_desc Source memory descriptor.
    8148              :         /// @param dst_desc Destination memory descriptor.
    8149              :         /// @param epsilon Batch normalization epsilon parameter.
    8150              :         /// @param flags Batch normalization flags (@ref
    8151              :         ///     dnnl::normalization_flags).
    8152              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
    8153              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
    8154              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    8155              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
    8156              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
    8157              :         ///     defaults to false.
    8158        89291 :         primitive_desc(const engine &aengine, prop_kind aprop_kind,
    8159              :                 const memory::desc &src_desc, const memory::desc &dst_desc,

    8161              :                 const primitive_attr &attr = default_attr(),
    8162       138422 :                 bool allow_empty = false) {
    8163        89291 :             dnnl_primitive_desc_t pd = nullptr;
    8164        88272 :             dnnl_status_t status
    8165        89291 :                     = dnnl_batch_normalization_forward_primitive_desc_create(
    8166              :                             &pd, aengine.get(), dnnl::convert_to_c(aprop_kind),
    8167        89291 :                             src_desc.get(), dst_desc.get(), epsilon,
    8168        89291 :                             convert_to_c(flags), attr.get());
    8169              : 
    8170        89291 :             if (!allow_empty)
    8171        79531 :                 error::wrap_c_api(status,
    8172              :                         "could not create a primitive descriptor for "

    8174              :                         "primitive. Run workload with environment variable "
    8175              :                         "ONEDNN_VERBOSE=all to get additional diagnostic "
    8176              :                         "information.");
    8177        40160 :             reset(pd);
    8178        40160 :         }

    8385              : /// @} dnnl_api_batch_normalization
    8386              : 
    8387              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_group_normalization Group Normalization
    8388              : ///
    8389              : /// A primitive to perform group normalization.
    8390              : ///
    8391              : /// Both forward and backward propagation primitives support in-place
    8392              : /// operation; that is, src and dst can refer to the same memory for forward
    8393              : /// propagation, and diff_dst and diff_src can refer to the same memory for
    8394              : /// backward propagation.
    8395              : ///
    8396              : /// The group normalization primitives computations can be controlled by
    8397              : /// specifying different @ref dnnl::normalization_flags values. For example,
    8398              : /// group normalization forward propagation can be configured to either
    8399              : /// compute the mean and variance or take them as arguments. It can either
    8400              : /// perform scaling and shifting using gamma and beta parameters or not.
    8401              : ///
    8402              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_group_normalization in developer guide
    8403              : ///
    8404              : /// @{
    8405              : 
    8406              : /// Group normalization forward propagation primitive.
    8407        30192 : struct group_normalization_forward : public primitive {
    8408              :     /// Primitive descriptor for a group normalization forward propagation
    8409              :     /// primitive.
    8410       175823 :     struct primitive_desc : public dnnl::primitive_desc {
    8411              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    8412        30239 :         primitive_desc() = default;
    8413              : 
    8414              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a group normalization forward
    8415              :         /// propagation primitive.
    8416              :         ///
    8417              :         /// @note
    8418              :         ///     In-place operation is supported: the dst can refer to the same
    8419              :         ///     memory as the src.
    8420              :         ///
    8421              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
    8422              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
    8423              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training and
    8424              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
    8425              :         /// @param src_desc Source memory descriptor.
    8426              :         /// @param dst_desc Destination memory descriptor.
    8427              :         /// @param groups Group normalization groups parameter.
    8428              :         /// @param epsilon Group normalization epsilon parameter.
    8429              :         /// @param flags Group normalization flags (@ref
    8430              :         ///     dnnl::normalization_flags).
    8431              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
    8432              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
    8433              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    8434              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
    8435              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
    8436              :         ///     defaults to false.

    8673              : /// @addtogroup dnnl_api_layer_normalization Layer Normalization
    8674              : ///
    8675              : /// A primitive to perform layer normalization. Normalization is performed
    8676              : /// within the last logical dimension of data tensor.
    8677              : ///
    8678              : /// Both forward and backward propagation primitives support in-place
    8679              : /// operation; that is, src and dst can refer to the same memory for forward
    8680              : /// propagation, and diff_dst and diff_src can refer to the same memory for
    8681              : /// backward propagation.
    8682              : ///
    8683              : /// The layer normalization primitives computations can be controlled by
    8684              : /// specifying different @ref dnnl::normalization_flags values. For example,
    8685              : /// layer normalization forward propagation can be configured to either
    8686              : /// compute the mean and variance or take them as arguments. It can either
    8687              : /// perform scaling and shifting using gamma and beta parameters or not.
    8688              : ///
    8689              : /// @sa @ref dev_guide_layer_normalization in developer guide
    8690              : ///
    8691              : /// @{
    8692              : 
    8693              : /// Layer normalization forward propagation primitive.
    8694        73188 : struct layer_normalization_forward : public primitive {
    8695              :     /// Primitive descriptor for a layer normalization forward propagation

    8698              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
    8699        73659 :         primitive_desc() = default;

    8707              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
    8708              :         /// @param src_desc Source memory descriptor.
    8709              :         /// @param dst_desc Destination memory descriptor.
    8710              :         /// @param stat_desc Statistics memory descriptors.
    8711              :         /// @param epsilon Layer normalization epsilon parameter.
    8712              :         /// @param flags Layer normalization flags (@ref
    8713              :         ///     dnnl::normalization_flags).
    8714              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
    8715              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
    8716              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
    8717              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
    8718              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
    8719              :         ///     defaults to false.

    9759              :     // (Deliberately not using doxygen comments)
    9760              :     //
    9761              :     // Constructs an RNN primitive descriptor base from a C API primitive
    9762              :     // descriptor while checking that it actually describes the expected
    9763              :     // primitive by comparing propagation and primitive kinds. Caller can
    9764              :     // pass two options propagation kinds. This is typically used to check
    9765              :     // that propagation kind is inference or training forward propagation.
    9766              :     //
    9767              :     // @param pd C API primitive descriptor.
    9768              :     // @param prop_kind1 Expected propagation kind.
    9769              :     // @param prop_kind2 Expected propagation kind.

    9771          517 :     rnn_primitive_desc_base(dnnl_primitive_desc_t pd,
    9772              :             dnnl::prop_kind prop_kind1, dnnl::prop_kind prop_kind2,
    9773          517 :             dnnl::algorithm cell_kind) {
    9774              : 
    9775          517 :         dnnl_status_t rc;
    9776              : 
    9777          517 :         dnnl_primitive_kind_t q_primitive_kind;
    9778          517 :         rc = dnnl_primitive_desc_query(
    9779              :                 pd, dnnl_query_primitive_kind, 0, &q_primitive_kind);
    9780          517 :         error::wrap_c_api(rc,
    9781              :                 "could not retrieve a primitive kind from a primitive "
    9782              :                 "descriptor for an RNN primitive");
    9783              : 
    9784          517 :         dnnl_prop_kind_t q_prop_kind;

   10091              : 
   10092              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a vanilla RNN forward
   10093              :         ///     propagation primitive.
   10094              :         ///
   10095              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   10096              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc,
   10097              :         /// - @p bias_desc,
   10098              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc.
   10099              :         ///
   10100              :         /// This would then indicate that the RNN forward propagation primitive
   10101              :         /// should not use them and should default to zero values instead.
   10102              :         ///
   10103              :         /// @note
   10104              :         ///     All memory descriptors except @p src_iter_desc can be
   10105              :         ///     initialized with an #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p
   10106              :         ///     format_tag.
   10107              :         ///
   10108              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   10109              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
   10110              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
   10111              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
   10112              :         /// @param activation Activation kind. Possible values are
   10113              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_relu,
   10114              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_tanh, or
   10115              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_logistic.
   10116              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   10117              :         ///     more info.
   10118              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   10119              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10120              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10121              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10122              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   10123              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   10124              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   10125              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   10126              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   10127              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10128              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10129              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   10130              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   10131              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is

   10134              :         ///     defaults to false.

   10153              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a vanilla RNN forward

   10300              : /// Vanilla RNN backward propagation primitive.
   10301              : struct vanilla_rnn_backward : public primitive {
   10302              :     /// Primitive descriptor for an RNN backward propagation primitive.
   10303              :     struct primitive_desc : public rnn_primitive_desc_base {
   10304              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
   10305              :         primitive_desc() = default;
   10306              : 
   10307              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a vanilla RNN backward
   10308              :         ///     propagation primitive.
   10309              :         ///
   10310              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   10311              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p diff_src_iter_desc,
   10312              :         /// - @p bias_desc together with @p diff_bias_desc,
   10313              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc together with @p diff_dst_iter_desc.
   10314              :         ///
   10315              :         /// This would then indicate that the RNN backward propagation
   10316              :         /// primitive should not use the respective data and should use zero
   10317              :         /// values instead.
   10318              :         ///
   10319              :         /// @note
   10320              :         ///     All the memory descriptors may be initialized with the
   10321              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
   10322              :         ///
   10323              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   10324              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Must be
   10325              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::backward.
   10326              :         /// @param activation Activation kind. Possible values are
   10327              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_relu,
   10328              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_tanh, or
   10329              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_logistic.
   10330              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   10331              :         ///     more info.
   10332              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   10333              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10334              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10335              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10336              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   10337              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   10338              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   10339              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   10340              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   10341              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10342              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10343              :         /// @param diff_src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   10344              :         ///     vector.
   10345              :         /// @param diff_src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   10346              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   10347              :         /// @param diff_weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10348              :         ///     weights applied to the layer input.
   10349              :         /// @param diff_weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10350              :         ///     weights applied to the recurrent input.
   10351              :         /// @param diff_bias_desc Diff bias memory descriptor.
   10352              :         /// @param diff_dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10353              :         ///     output vector.
   10354              :         /// @param diff_dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of output
   10355              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   10356              :         /// @param hint_fwd_pd Primitive descriptor for a vanilla RNN
   10357              :         ///     forward propagation primitive. It is used as a hint for
   10358              :         ///     deciding which memory format to use.
   10359              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   10360              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   10361              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   10362              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   10363              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and

   10395              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a vanilla RNN backward
   10396              :         ///     propagation primitive with an alpha parameter.
   10397              :         ///
   10398              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   10399              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p diff_src_iter_desc,
   10400              :         /// - @p bias_desc together with @p diff_bias_desc,
   10401              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc together with @p diff_dst_iter_desc.
   10402              :         ///
   10403              :         /// This would then indicate that the RNN backward propagation
   10404              :         /// primitive should not use the respective data and should use zero
   10405              :         /// values instead.
   10406              :         ///
   10407              :         /// @note
   10408              :         ///     All the memory descriptors may be initialized with the
   10409              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
   10410              :         ///
   10411              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   10412              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Must be
   10413              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::backward.
   10414              :         /// @param activation Activation kind. Possible values are
   10415              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_relu,
   10416              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_tanh, or
   10417              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_logistic.
   10418              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   10419              :         ///     more info.
   10420              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   10421              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10422              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10423              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10424              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   10425              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   10426              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   10427              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   10428              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   10429              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10430              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10431              :         /// @param diff_src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   10432              :         ///     vector.
   10433              :         /// @param diff_src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   10434              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   10435              :         /// @param diff_weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10436              :         ///     weights applied to the layer input.
   10437              :         /// @param diff_weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10438              :         ///     weights applied to the recurrent input.
   10439              :         /// @param diff_bias_desc Diff bias memory descriptor.
   10440              :         /// @param diff_dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10441              :         ///     output vector.
   10442              :         /// @param diff_dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of output
   10443              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   10444              :         /// @param alpha Negative slope if activation is
   10445              :         ///     #dnnl::algorithm::eltwise_relu.
   10446              :         /// @param hint_fwd_pd Primitive descriptor for a vanilla RNN
   10447              :         ///     forward propagation primitive. It is used as a hint for
   10448              :         ///     deciding which memory format to use.
   10449              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   10450              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   10451              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   10452              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   10453              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
   10454              :         ///     defaults to false.

   10603              : /// LSTM forward propagation primitive.
   10604          780 : struct lstm_forward : public primitive {
   10605              :     /// Primitive descriptor for an LSTM forward propagation primitive.

   10607              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.

   10609              : 
   10610              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for an LSTM (with or without
   10611              :         ///     peephole and with or without projection) forward propagation
   10612              :         ///     primitive.
   10613              :         ///
   10614              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   10615              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p src_iter_c_desc,
   10616              :         /// - @p weights_peephole_desc,
   10617              :         /// - @p bias_desc,
   10618              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc together with @p dst_iter_c_desc.
   10619              :         ///
   10620              :         /// This would then indicate that the LSTM forward propagation
   10621              :         /// primitive should not use them and should default to zero values
   10622              :         /// instead.
   10623              :         ///
   10624              :         /// The @p weights_projection_desc may point to a zero memory
   10625              :         /// descriptor. This would then indicate that the LSTM doesn't have
   10626              :         /// recurrent projection layer.
   10627              :         ///
   10628              :         /// @note
   10629              :         ///     All memory descriptors can be initialized with an
   10630              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
   10631              :         ///
   10632              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   10633              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
   10634              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
   10635              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
   10636              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   10637              :         ///     more info.
   10638              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   10639              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10640              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10641              :         /// @param src_iter_c_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10642              :         ///     cell state vector.
   10643              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10644              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   10645              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   10646              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   10647              :         /// @param weights_peephole_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10648              :         ///     applied to the cell states (according to the Peephole LSTM
   10649              :         ///     formula).
   10650              :         /// @param weights_projection_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10651              :         ///     applied to the hidden states to get the recurrent projection
   10652              :         ///     (according to the Projection LSTM formula).
   10653              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   10654              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   10655              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10656              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10657              :         /// @param dst_iter_c_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10658              :         ///     cell state vector.
   10659              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional

   10661              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   10662              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   10663              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
   10664              :         ///     defaults to false.

   10756              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for an LSTM forward propagation
   10757              :         ///     primitive.
   10758              :         ///
   10759              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   10760              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p src_iter_c_desc,
   10761              :         /// - @p bias_desc,
   10762              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc together with @p dst_iter_c_desc.

   10764              :         /// This would then indicate that the LSTM forward propagation

   10767              :         ///
   10768              :         /// @note
   10769              :         ///     All memory descriptors can be initialized with an
   10770              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
   10771              :         ///
   10772              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   10773              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
   10774              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
   10775              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.

   10914           96 :     struct primitive_desc : public rnn_primitive_desc_base {
   10915              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
   10916              :         primitive_desc() = default;
   10917              : 
   10918              :         /// Constructs an LSTM (with or without peephole and with or without
   10919              :         ///     projection) primitive descriptor for backward propagation
   10920              :         ///     using @p prop_kind, @p direction, and memory descriptors.
   10921              :         ///
   10922              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   10923              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p src_iter_c_desc,
   10924              :         ///   @p diff_src_iter_desc, and @p diff_src_iter_c_desc,
   10925              :         /// - @p weights_peephole_desc together with
   10926              :         ///   @p diff_weights_peephole_desc
   10927              :         /// - @p bias_desc together with @p diff_bias_desc,
   10928              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc together with @p dst_iter_c_desc,
   10929              :         ///   @p diff_dst_iter_desc, and @p diff_dst_iter_c_desc.
   10930              :         ///
   10931              :         /// This would then indicate that the LSTM backward propagation
   10932              :         /// primitive should not use them and should default to zero values
   10933              :         /// instead.
   10934              :         ///
   10935              :         /// The @p weights_projection_desc together with @p
   10936              :         /// diff_weights_projection_desc may point to a zero memory descriptor.
   10937              :         /// This would then indicate that the LSTM doesn't have recurrent
   10938              :         /// projection layer.
   10939              :         ///
   10940              :         /// @note
   10941              :         ///     All memory descriptors can be initialized with
   10942              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
   10943              :         ///
   10944              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   10945              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Must be
   10946              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::backward.
   10947              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   10948              :         ///     more info.
   10949              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   10950              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10951              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10952              :         /// @param src_iter_c_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   10953              :         ///     cell state vector.
   10954              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10955              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   10956              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   10957              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   10958              :         /// @param weights_peephole_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10959              :         ///     applied to the cell states (according to the Peephole LSTM
   10960              :         ///     formula).
   10961              :         /// @param weights_projection_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   10962              :         ///     applied to the hidden states to get the recurrent projection
   10963              :         ///     (according to the Projection LSTM formula).
   10964              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   10965              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   10966              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10967              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   10968              :         /// @param dst_iter_c_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   10969              :         ///     cell state vector.
   10970              :         /// @param diff_src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   10971              :         ///     vector.
   10972              :         /// @param diff_src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   10973              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   10974              :         /// @param diff_src_iter_c_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10975              :         ///     input recurrent cell state vector.
   10976              :         /// @param diff_weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10977              :         ///     weights applied to the layer input.
   10978              :         /// @param diff_weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10979              :         ///     weights applied to the recurrent input.
   10980              :         /// @param diff_weights_peephole_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10981              :         ///     weights applied to the cell states (according to the Peephole
   10982              :         ///     LSTM formula).
   10983              :         /// @param diff_weights_projection_desc Memory descriptor for the diff
   10984              :         ///     of weights applied to the hidden states to get the recurrent
   10985              :         ///     projection (according to the Projection LSTM formula).
   10986              :         /// @param diff_bias_desc Diff bias memory descriptor.
   10987              :         /// @param diff_dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10988              :         ///     output vector.
   10989              :         /// @param diff_dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of output
   10990              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   10991              :         /// @param diff_dst_iter_c_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   10992              :         ///     output recurrent cell state vector.
   10993              :         /// @param hint_fwd_pd Primitive descriptor for an LSTM
   10994              :         ///     forward propagation primitive. It is used as a hint for
   10995              :         ///     deciding which memory format to use.
   10996              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   10997              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   10998              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   10999              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   11000              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
   11001              :         ///     defaults to false.

   11154              :         ///     using @p prop_kind, @p direction, and memory descriptors.
   11155              :         ///
   11156              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   11157              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p src_iter_c_desc,

   11167              :         /// @note

   11170              :         ///

   11172              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Must be

   11403              : 
   11404              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a GRU forward propagation
   11405              :         ///     primitive.
   11406              :         ///
   11407              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   11408              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc,
   11409              :         /// - @p bias_desc,
   11410              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc.
   11411              :         ///
   11412              :         /// This would then indicate that the GRU forward propagation primitive
   11413              :         /// should not use them and should default to zero values instead.
   11414              :         ///
   11415              :         /// @note
   11416              :         ///     All memory descriptors except @p src_iter_desc may be
   11417              :         ///     initialized with an #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p
   11418              :         ///     format_tag.
   11419              :         ///
   11420              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   11421              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
   11422              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
   11423              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
   11424              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   11425              :         ///     more info.
   11426              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   11427              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   11428              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   11429              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   11430              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   11431              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   11432              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   11433              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   11434              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   11435              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   11436              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   11437              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   11438              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   11439              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   11440              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   11441              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
   11442              :         ///     defaults to false.

   11532              : /// GRU backward propagation primitive.
   11533              : struct gru_backward : public primitive {
   11534              :     /// Primitive descriptor for a GRU backward propagation primitive.
   11535              :     struct primitive_desc : public rnn_primitive_desc_base {
   11536              :         /// Default constructor. Produces an empty object.
   11537              :         primitive_desc() = default;
   11538              : 
   11539              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for a GRU backward propagation
   11540              :         ///     primitive.
   11541              :         ///
   11542              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   11543              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc together with @p diff_src_iter_desc,
   11544              :         /// - @p bias_desc together with @p diff_bias_desc,
   11545              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc together with @p diff_dst_iter_desc.
   11546              :         ///
   11547              :         /// This would then indicate that the GRU backward propagation
   11548              :         /// primitive should not use them and should default to zero values
   11549              :         /// instead.
   11550              :         ///
   11551              :         /// @note
   11552              :         ///     All memory descriptors may be initialized with
   11553              :         ///     #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p format_tag.
   11554              :         ///
   11555              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   11556              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Must be
   11557              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::backward.
   11558              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   11559              :         ///     more info.
   11560              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   11561              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   11562              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   11563              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   11564              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   11565              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   11566              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   11567              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   11568              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   11569              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   11570              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   11571              :         /// @param diff_src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   11572              :         ///     vector.
   11573              :         /// @param diff_src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of input
   11574              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   11575              :         /// @param diff_weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   11576              :         ///     weights applied to the layer input.
   11577              :         /// @param diff_weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   11578              :         ///     weights applied to the recurrent input.
   11579              :         /// @param diff_bias_desc Diff bias memory descriptor.
   11580              :         /// @param diff_dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of
   11581              :         ///     output vector.
   11582              :         /// @param diff_dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the diff of output
   11583              :         ///     recurrent hidden state vector.
   11584              :         /// @param hint_fwd_pd Primitive descriptor for a GRU
   11585              :         ///     forward propagation primitive. It is used as a hint for
   11586              :         ///     deciding which memory format to use.
   11587              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   11588              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   11589              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   11590              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   11591              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
   11592              :         ///     defaults to false.
   11593              :         primitive_desc(const engine &aengine, prop_kind aprop_kind,
   11594              :                 rnn_direction direction, const memory::desc &src_layer_desc,
   11595              :                 const memory::desc &src_iter_desc,
   11596              :                 const memory::desc &weights_layer_desc,
   11597              :                 const memory::desc &weights_iter_desc,
   11598              :                 const memory::desc &bias_desc,
   11599              :                 const memory::desc &dst_layer_desc,
   11600              :                 const memory::desc &dst_iter_desc,
   11601              :                 const memory::desc &diff_src_layer_desc,
   11602              :                 const memory::desc &diff_src_iter_desc,
   11603              :                 const memory::desc &diff_weights_layer_desc,
   11604              :                 const memory::desc &diff_weights_iter_desc,
   11605              :                 const memory::desc &diff_bias_desc,
   11606              :                 const memory::desc &diff_dst_layer_desc,
   11607              :                 const memory::desc &diff_dst_iter_desc,
   11608              :                 const gru_forward::primitive_desc &hint_fwd_pd,
   11609              :                 const primitive_attr &attr = default_attr(),

   11734              : 
   11735              :         /// Constructs a primitive descriptor for LBR GRU forward propagation
   11736              :         ///     primitive.
   11737              :         ///
   11738              :         /// The following arguments may point to a zero memory descriptor:
   11739              :         /// - @p src_iter_desc,
   11740              :         /// - @p bias_desc,
   11741              :         /// - @p dst_iter_desc.
   11742              :         ///
   11743              :         /// This would then indicate that the LBR GRU forward propagation
   11744              :         /// primitive should not use them and should default to zero values
   11745              :         /// instead.
   11746              :         ///
   11747              :         /// @note
   11748              :         ///     All memory descriptors except @p src_iter_desc may be
   11749              :         ///     initialized with an #dnnl::memory::format_tag::any value of @p
   11750              :         ///     format_tag.
   11751              :         ///
   11752              :         /// @param aengine Engine to use.
   11753              :         /// @param aprop_kind Propagation kind. Possible values are
   11754              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_training, and
   11755              :         ///     #dnnl::prop_kind::forward_inference.
   11756              :         /// @param direction RNN direction. See @ref dnnl::rnn_direction for
   11757              :         ///     more info.
   11758              :         /// @param src_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the input vector.
   11759              :         /// @param src_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the input recurrent
   11760              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   11761              :         /// @param weights_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the weights
   11762              :         ///     applied to the layer input.
   11763              :         /// @param weights_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the weights applied
   11764              :         ///     to the recurrent input.
   11765              :         /// @param bias_desc Bias memory descriptor.
   11766              :         /// @param dst_layer_desc Memory descriptor for the output vector.
   11767              :         /// @param dst_iter_desc Memory descriptor for the output recurrent
   11768              :         ///     hidden state vector.
   11769              :         /// @param attr Primitive attributes to use. Attributes are optional
   11770              :         ///     and default to empty attributes.
   11771              :         /// @param allow_empty A flag signifying whether construction is
   11772              :         ///     allowed to fail without throwing an exception. In this case an
   11773              :         ///     empty object will be produced. This flag is optional and
   11774              :         ///     defaults to false.
